title: ficus tinctoria var. neoehudarum
intro: Ficus tinctoria var. neoehudarum, also known as the Indian laurel, is a type of plant native to tropical Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and aiding in digestion. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Ficus tinctoria var. neoehudarum are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and powders that can be used to treat various ailments.
